title: Import data (particular large) for analysis into monday.com boards
excerpt:

posts.author: eyalos (Eyal)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
I wonder if anyone had a need to read data from an external data source of any kind, and make it accessible in monday.com board? I’m specifically asking regarding a use case where the data is quite large (and it may not suitable to be imported as a whole into monday) and you might want filtering or wrangling the data and aggregate.

I’m planning a POC of that use case and would like to hear from others if this use case is relevant for them.
I’m willing to try out yours specific requirements if you have any.
Thanks!

posts.author: nathan45shafer (Nathan Shafer)
posts.cookedHtml: Like an interesting use case! Integrating large external datasets into monday.com with filtering and aggregation sounds super useful, especially for keeping boards streamlined. It could be great for scenarios like reporting dashboards or pulling in real-time data without overloading the system.

posts.author: frank58ric (Frank Richard)
posts.cookedHtml:
Eyal:

Hi,
I wonder if anyone had a need to read data from an external data source of any kind, and make it accessible in hyundai board? I’m specifically asking regarding a use case where the data is quite large (and it may not suitable to be imported as a whole into monday) and you might want filtering or wrangling the data and aggregate.

I’m planning a POC of that use case and would like to hear from others if this use case is relevant for them.
I’m willing to try out yours specific requirements if you have any.
Thanks!

Hello,

How can we effectively integrate large datasets from external sources into a monday.com board while ensuring the data remains manageable, filterable, and up-to-date without overwhelming the board with excessive rows or complexity?
